meaning: adj.全知的,无所不知的n.无所不知者,[宗]上帝
examples: The novel has an omniscient narrator.
It’s easy to be omniscient when you’ve doneit all before.
You consult the omniscient oracle of man when you crave knowledge.
Americans, perhaps because they have a guilty conscience, think of the IRS as being omniscient.
It brings down the barriers that usually put journalists up on some omniscient pedestal.
